perturbation.original_text: (a) In full consideration of the provision of the services of the Blockchain Administrator set forth herein, the Fund shall pay the Blockchain Administrator a fees calculated at the annual rate of 0.20% of the value of the Fund's average annual net assets. Such fee shall be accrued daily and paid monthly in arrears.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) In full consideration of the provision of the services of the Blockchain Administrator set forth herein, the Fund shall pay the Blockchain Administrator a fees calculated at the annual rate of 0.20% of the value of the Fund's average annual net assets. Such fee shall be accrued daily and paid quarterly in advance.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the payment schedule for the Blockchain Administrator's fees. The original text specifies that fees are paid monthly in arrears, while the modified text states that fees are paid quarterly in advance. This creates a contradiction because the contract now contains two conflicting statements regarding how frequently and when the Blockchain Administrator will receive payment. This contradicts Section 7 which specifies the original amount is owed through the termination date. The contradiction appears because there is no specification if the payments will be refunded upon termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) This Agreement shall become effective as of the first date above written. This Agreement may be terminated at any time, without the payment of any penalty, upon 60 days' written notice, by the vote of a majority of the outstanding voting securities of the Fund or by the vote of the Fund's Trustees or by the Blockchain Administrator. The provisions of Section 5 of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect, and the Blockchain Administrator shall remain entitled to the benefits thereof, notwithstanding any termination of this Agreement. Further, notwithstanding the termination or expiration of this Agreement as aforesaid, the Blockchain Administrator shall be entitled to any amounts owed under Section 4 through the date of termination or expiration, and Section 5 shall continue in force and effect and apply to the Blockchain Administrator and its representatives as and to the extent applicable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment schedule from 'monthly in arrears' to 'quarterly in advance.' This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which states the Blockchain Administrator is entitled to amounts owed under Section 4 (which now specifies quarterly in advance payments) 'through the date of termination or expiration.' The absence of a refund clause for advance payments upon termination creates a contradiction because the Fund could potentially overpay if the agreement terminates before the end of a quarter, and the original agreement had no chance of overpayment because it was paid in arrears. This directly breaks compatibility within the document, particularly regarding financial obligations upon termination.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated at any time, without the payment of any penalty, upon 60 days' written notice, by the vote of a majority of the outstanding voting securities of the Fund or by the vote of the Fund's Trustees or by the Blockchain Administrator.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated at any time, with a termination fee of $10,000, upon 60 days' written notice, by the vote of a majority of the outstanding voting securities of the Fund or by the vote of the Fund's Trustees or by the Blockchain Administrator.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces a direct in-text contradiction by adding a termination fee of $10,000 while the original text explicitly states that there is no penalty for termination. This creates uncertainty about the actual terms of termination, leading to potential disputes. This contradicts with the limitation of liability in Section 5, which refers back to the duties and obligations under this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Blockchain Administrator (and its officers, managers, partners, agents, employees, controlling persons, members and any other person or entity affiliated with the Blockchain Administrator) shall not be liable to the Fund for any action taken or omitted to be taken by the Blockchain Administrator in connection with the performance of any of its duties or obligations under this Agreement or otherwise as blockchain administrator of the Fund, and the Fund shall indemnify, defend and protect the Blockchain Administrator (and its officers, managers, partners, agents, employees, controlling persons, members and any other person or entity affiliated with the Blockchain Administrator) (collectively, the "Indemnified Parties") and hold them harmless from and against all damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees and amounts reasonably paid in settlement) incurred by the Indemnified Parties in or by reason of any pending, threatened or completed action, suit, investigation or other proceeding (including an action or suit by or in the right of the Fund or its security holders) arising out of or otherwise based upon the performance of any of the Blockchain Administrator's duties or obligations under this Agreement or otherwise as administrator of the Fund. Notwithstanding the preceding sentence of this Section 5 to the contrary, nothing contained herein shall protect or be deemed to protect the Indemnified Parties against or entitle or be deemed to entitle the Indemnified Parties to indemnification in respect of, any liability to the Fund or its security holders to which the Indemnified Parties would otherwise be subject by reason of criminal conduct, willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence in the performance of the Blockchain Administrator's duties or by reason of the reckless disregard of the Blockchain Administrator's duties and obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the agreement could be terminated without any penalty. The changed text introduces a $10,000 termination fee. This direct conflict creates a contradiction. Moreover, Section 5 limits the liability of the Blockchain Administrator based on their duties under the agreement. If a termination fee is now applicable, this creates a contradiction to the limitations of liability in Section 5 because the administrator's actions regarding termination, including any disputes over the fee, would now be subject to potential liability, conflicting with the original intent of limiting liability to instances of criminal conduct, willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence. Therefore, the introduction of the termination fee directly contradicts both the original 'no penalty' clause and the limitations outlined in Section 5.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be amended pursuant to a written instrument by mutual consent of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended pursuant to a written instrument by unilateral consent of the Blockchain Administrator.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction in section 9. The original text requires mutual consent for amendments, ensuring both parties agree to changes. The changed text allows the Blockchain Administrator to unilaterally amend the agreement, contradicting the principle of mutual agreement. This can affect the other party's rights and obligations under any section of the agreement. This is contradicting since Section 10 states that this is the entire agreement of both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings and arrangements with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York applicable to contracts formed and to be performed entirely within the State of New York, without regard to conflict of laws principles, and in accordance with the applicable provisions of the Investment Fund Act. In such case, to the extent the applicable laws of the State of New York, or any of the provisions herein, conflict with the provisions of the Investment Fund Act, the latter shall control.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it allows the Blockchain Administrator to unilaterally amend the agreement, whereas Section 10, the 'Contradicted Text,' states that the agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties. The original text requires mutual consent for amendments, ensuring both parties agree to changes. The unilateral amendment power granted to the Blockchain Administrator fundamentally undermines the principle of mutual agreement and the entire agreement of both parties as laid out in the contradicted text, which establishes that the contract represents the complete understanding between all parties involved. This directly conflicts with the notion of a complete, mutually agreed-upon understanding if one party can unilaterally alter the agreement's terms.
